titleStmt.title: Zheng Xi shang bei (Zheng Wengong stele) (rubbing)
鄭羲上碑 / 魏故中書令祕書監鄭文公之碑（拓本）

msIdentifier.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

msContents.summary: Ink rubbing of a Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 Daoist cliff inscription: Zheng Xi shang bei (Zheng Wengong stele). GENUINE Zheng Daozhao. Mountainside inscription from Tianzhu shan, Shandong, extolling his father Zheng Xi (426-93). Part of the 鄭道昭碑全集 series. Held UC Berkeley C.V. Starr East Asian Library, Chinese Rubbings Collection (Mitsui/Chohyokaku provenance); call-number cluster Hsin148. Image is very high-res (12928x31611 px). IIIF Presentation v3 manifest verified.

support.objectType: 拓片 · ink rubbing on paper

origin.origDate: Northern Wei, Yongping period (511); creator field 鄭道昭撰並書. This rubbing preserves only 19 lines of the original; carries a Meiji 36 (1903) collation inscription.

bibl.ref: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley — record

revisionDesc.change: Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 Daoist cliff inscription; harvested from C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley via the Epiwen multi-repository workflow. IIIF deep-zoom, open access (CORS-enabled).

div.p: Ink rubbing — see the linked record/manifest.
